pi_items.source: Calendar verification: January 6, 2025 is a real, valid Monday date
Task description specifies 24 Angus cow-calf pairs; 'head' is standard livestock counting unit from USDA/Extension sources (wyoextension.org, beef.unl.edu)
USDA/Extension agriculture sources confirm 'head' is the standard unit for counting livestock in grazing operations
Realistic pasture naming convention; plausible for grazing rotation management
Realistic pasture naming convention; plausible for grazing rotation management
USDA Extension guidance (ME stageofgrowth.pdf) specifies cool-season grass pastures grazed to 3 inches residual for first spring grazing is valid; 1.5 inches is standard for regular season
USDA Extension guidance specifies legume-dominant pastures should be grazed from initial forage height of 8-10 inches; 8 inches at entry is verified standard
Oxford Academic Journal of Animal Science: Angus and Brangus cow-calf pairs were rotationally grazed at 14-day intervals in research studies; verified practice
Calendar verification: January 20, 2025 is exactly 14 days after January 6, 2025 (both Mondays); mathematically and logically consistent with 14-day grazing rotation

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic UI interaction task for the farmOS Field Kit mobile app. The task requires creating an Activity log entry documenting a livestock grazing rotation move. All metadata values are grounded in real agricultural practices: January 6, 2025 (Monday) and January 20, 2025 (Monday) represent a validated 14-day grazing rotation interval used for Angus cow-calf pairs in rotational grazing studies. The quantity of 24 head refers to 24 Angus cow-calf pairs. Forage heights of 8 inches at entry and 3 inches residual are documented USDA Extension guidelines for rotational grazing management. The 14-day grazing period is validated by Oxford Academic research on Angus grazing performance. All required phrases represent realistic grazing rotation management details that are consistent with established best practices in livestock management.
